title: Coleus omissus
taxonomy: Coleus omissus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coleus, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Coleus omissus is further classified as Coleus omissus (J.Koenig ex Roxb.) Benth., with Benth. referring to the botanist George Bentham who reclassified the plant. The classification of Coleus omissus reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants within the Lamiaceae family. Coleus omissus is a relatively rare and understudied plant species.

intro:
There is limited information available about the Coleus omissus plant, however, I'll provide information about Coleus plants in general. 

The Coleus plant is a genus of flowering plants in the family Lamiaceae, characterized by their colorful, leafy foliage and ability to thrive in warm, humid environments. Coleus plants have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Coleus plants include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coleus plants include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
